preview_text:
The model assigns Belgium a 50% chance of victory, making them the statistical favorite, while Egypt's win probability stands at 20%. A draw carries a 30% likelihood under the model's estimate, suggesting this match has genuine competitive uncertainty built into the forecast.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects a moderately competitive fixture. Belgium's edge is real but not commanding; Egypt remains capable of producing a result. The 30% draw probability indicates the teams are close enough in quality that a stalemate is a plausible outcome rather than an outlier possibility.

The sportsbook consensus elevates Belgium to 56% and reduces Egypt to 19%, a 6-point shift in Belgium's favor compared to the model. The draw contracts to 24%. The two sources diverge meaningfully on Belgium's chances; the market is more confident in the favorite than the statistical model. The dashboard does not adjudicate between them.
